news.summary: Qatar announces a substantial investment of $1 billion over the span of 10 years in cutting-edge quantum technologies and workforce development. This funding will be channeled through a joint venture to support academic and industrial partners. The initiative, spearheaded by Quantinuum based in DOHA, Qatar, aims to solidify their position as the global leader in quantum computing. The partnership is set to boost research and innovation in this field significantly.
news.headline: Quantinuum and Al Rabban Capital partner for $1B investment over 10 years in quantum computing in Qatar and the region.

news.summary: Jeffy Yu, the developer of Zerebro, was found alive at his parents' home in San Francisco after faking his suicide on a livestream which propelled a memecoin, LLJEFFY, to a market cap of nearly $105 million before plummeting to $5.63 million. Yu claimed the stunt was a response to harassment. In another case, Hu Lezhi, a Chinese programmer, burned $1.3 million worth of Ether and made allegations of mind control by a hedge fund using "brain-computer weapons" before disappearing. DeFi developer Nikolai Mushegian's mysterious death raised suspicions of foul play in the crypto community. QuadrigaCX founder Gerald Cotten's death left investors unable to access $190 million in crypto, leading to bankruptcy suspicions of a Ponzi scheme. Lastly, OneCoin fraudster Ruja Ignatova disappeared, with rumors suggesting murder, plastic surgery, or living under protection of the Bulgarian mafia. The FBI raised a $5 million reward for her capture. These cases highlight the complex and often unsettling nature of the crypto world.
news.headline: Five crypto figures disappeared, died, or deceived us, from fake suicides to vanishing acts. Notable cases include Jeffy Yu's $100 million memecoin stunt and Gerald Cotten's mysterious $190 million crypto access after his reported death in 2018. With stories of paranoia, possible mind control, and even an alleged $4-billion scam founder's disappearance, the crypto world is no stranger to unsettling mysteries.

news.summary: Biodesix, Inc. (NASDAQ: BDSX) reported less favorable first-quarter results on Tuesday. The company's quarterly losses came in at 8 cents per share, falling short of the analyst consensus of 7 cents per share. Meanwhile, quarterly sales totaled $17.96 million, also below the analyst consensus of $19.47 million. Scott Hutton, the CEO, commented, "Our focus on achieving our strategic goals for 2025 led to a 21% revenue growth compared to last year. We managed to boost revenue through increased lung diagnostic testing and diagnostic services, all while reducing SG&A spending."
news.headline: Biodesix (NASDAQ: BDSX) reports Q1 losses at 8 cents/share, missing analyst estimates. Sales at $17.96M vs $19.47M estimate. CEO remains optimistic.

news.summary: In Fargo, North Dakota on May 14, 2025, Checkable Health is advocating for regulatory reform following years of limited progress in transitioning prescription medications to over-the-counter status. The company is known for potentially introducing the initial FDA-approved strep test for at-home use. This call for change comes after a period of stagnation in the Rx-to-OTC innovation sector. The initiative has been prompted by recent developments and is part of a larger push for more streamlined procedures.
news.headline: Checkable Health advocates for Rx-to-OTC reform after FDA-approved strep test breakthrough; aims to eliminate regulatory bottlenecks.

news.summary: A groundbreaking report on next generation giving by Christian Millennials was recently unveiled by the National Christian Foundation (NCF) in Alpharetta, Georgia. The report, released on May 14, 2025, marks the first of its kind and provides valuable insights into the giving habits of young Christians. This initiative comes as a part of NCF's efforts to study and understand the trends in philanthropy among the younger demographic. The report is a significant step forward in shedding light on the charitable contributions of Christian Millennials and aims to inform future strategies for charitable organizations.
news.headline: Report reveals Christian Millennials' giving perspectives as $124 trillion expected in wealth transfer.

The global market for these solutions had a value of US$54.4 billion in 2024 and is estimated to reach US$148.6 billion by the year 2030, indicating a growth rate of 18.2% between 2024 and 2030. The report offers detailed insights into market trends, drivers, and future predictions to assist in making strategic business choices. This comprehensive analysis serves as a valuable resource for industry stakeholders.

news.summary: In San Francisco, a recent funding announcement revealed that TurbineOne, a defense software company specializing in real-time threat detection and decision-making at the tactical edge, has successfully raised $36 million in a Series B round. The investment was led by The General Partnership and saw participation from Bessemer Venture Partners, XYZ Ventures, Stepstone Group, PROOF VC, and Artisanal Ventures. Following this round, the company is now valued at $300 million.

news.summary: On May 14, 2025, in Rye, New York, The Gabelli Multimedia Trust Inc.'s Board of Directors announced the approval of their fixed monthly cash distributions policy. The Fund, listed on NYSE as GGT, declared specific cash distributions for July, August, and September 2025. The approved distributions will be made during these three months as part of the Fund's ongoing commitment to providing fixed monthly cash payments to its shareholders.
news.headline: Gabelli Multimedia Trust (NYSE: GGT) maintains $0.88 per share annual distribution, with fixed monthly cash payouts approved for July, August, and September 2025.

news.summary: Tanim Rasul, COO of Canadian crypto exchange NDAX, criticized Canada's classification of stablecoins as securities in 2022, advocating for a payment instrument approach like Europe. The Canadian Securities Administrators (CSA) designated stablecoins as securities/derivatives due to market events, impacting top crypto companies like Binance and Gemini. Nevertheless, the local crypto industry thrived, generating $224 million in 2024 with an 18.6% CAGR, projected to hit $617.5 million by 2030. Stablecoins, valued at $242.8 billion, are gaining global traction, prompting nations to regulate. The stablecoin market continues to grow, emphasizing the need for a global regulatory framework.
news.headline: NDAX CO: Canada "wrong" to classify stablecoins as securities, should regulate as payment instruments. Canadian crypto industry revenue hits $224M in 2024, expected to reach $617.5M by 2030. Stablecoin market cap at $242.8B, up 51.9% from last year.

news.summary: Bitcoin's realized cap has increased by $30 billion since April 20, reaching $900 billion, showing steady investor confidence and new capital inflows. A pattern suggests a potential 10% breakout by next week. Glassnode reported positive market dynamics, with the Bitcoin spot volume delta peaking near $5 billion on May 13, indicating strong spot market conviction driving the rally. Despite growth slower than in late 2024, the current market sentiment remains positive. Bitcoin has been consolidating since early April, with a pattern of sideways movement followed by breakouts to higher ranges. The current consolidation phase has Bitcoin ranging between $100,678 and $105,700, with potential to retest $100,000-$102,000 before breaking above $110,000. However, a bearish scenario could emerge if prices drop below $102,000. This article provides analysis and insights, not investment advice. Readers are advised to conduct their own research before making financial decisions.
news.headline: Bitcoin bulls target new highs with capital inflows spiking. Realized cap grows $30B since April 20, hitting $900B now. 10% potential breakout next week.

news.summary: Thompson Thrift, a renowned real estate company, marked a significant milestone today in Indianapolis by commencing construction on The Union at Fishers District. news.summary: A recent report from RedStone Oracles suggests that Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is not a suitable benchmark for liquid funds due to its extreme volatility. The report, written by Marcin Kaźmierczak, argues that although Bitcoin has shown impressive returns, its risk-adjusted performance does not qualify it as a standard for assessing the success of capital preservation strategies. The analysis indicates that Bitcoin's Sharpe ratio has fluctuated between -6.58 and 6.97, with 25% of observations falling below -1.20, indicating high capital risk and inconsistency. These characteristics are contrary to the primary objectives of liquid funds, which prioritize stability, capital efficiency, and liquidity. The report highlights issues such as volatility and risk appetite as factors that make Bitcoin unsuitable as a benchmark for measuring the performance of liquid funds.
news.headline: Bitcoin's volatility makes it unsuitable as a benchmark for liquid funds, according to a report from RedStone Oracles. Bitcoin's Sharpe ratio ranged from -6.58 to 6.97, with 25% falling below -1.20. High risk and inconsistency hinder its use for measuring capital preservation strategies.

news.summary: On 14 May 2025, Reykjavik-based Kaldvik AS, with the ticker KLDVK, approved its annual report for 2024. The following adjustments were made compared to the Q4 report: the cost of materials decreased from EUR 68.4 million to EUR 67.9 million, other operating expenses decreased from EUR 24.4 million to EUR 24.2 million. Due to unfavorable currency fluctuations, the parent company, Kaldvik AS, now has a tax payable of EUR 0.5 million. For additional details, please reach out to Robert Robertsson, the CFO, at robert.robertsson@kaldvik.is. Kaldvik AS is a prominent salmon farming company in Iceland with a fully integrated value chain from hatchery to sales. They are listed on both Euronext Growth Oslo and First North Iceland Growth Market. Visit https://www.kaldvik.is for more information about the company.
news.headline: Kaldvik AS annual report for 2024 approved with changes: Cost of materials EURm 67.9, Other op. exp. EURm 24.2, tax payable EURm 0.5 due to currency fluctuations. Leading salmon farmer in Iceland.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was disclosed that the country's economy showcased remarkable growth in the last quarter, with a GDP increase of 4.5%. This positive trend was driven by a surge in consumer spending, which rose by 6% compared to the previous quarter. Additionally, exports saw a significant uptick, increasing by 8% and contributing to the overall economic expansion. The unemployment rate also dropped to 4%, marking a substantial improvement from the previous 6% rate. Analysts are optimistic about the future economic outlook, citing these figures as evidence of a strong and resilient economy.
news.headline: Coinbase CEO believes S&P inclusion confirms crypto's permanence, important step in market acceptance.

news.summary: A new product has been introduced by TIMBY Specialty Insurance Solutions, an inland marine MGU based in Scottsdale, Arizona. The Warehouse Legal Liability Coverage (WHLL) is aimed at bolstering their existing offerings for the logistics and transportation industries. This new initiative was unveiled on May 14, 2025.
news.headline: TIMBY Specialty Insurance Solutions introduces Warehouse Legal Liability Coverage on May 14, 2025, expanding their inland marine insurance services for logistics and transportation sectors.

news.summary: In Williamsport, Maryland on May 14, 2025, as the winter chill gives way to warmer weather, energy consumption tends to rise with the transition to summer. This shift can lead to heightened costs as cooling systems gear up to maintain optimal indoor temperatures. Potomac Edison, a subsidiary company, is set to address these seasonal energy challenges with measures to ensure efficient energy usage.
news.headline: Potomac Edison offers programs to help Maryland customers manage higher energy costs as summer approaches.

news.summary: Coca-Cola Beverages Florida, LLC (Coke Florida) based in Tampa, Florida, has achieved the prestigious recognition as a 2025 US Best Managed Companies Gold Standard Winner. This accolade, presented by Deloitte Private and The Wall Street Journal, is reserved for companies that have been consistently recognized for four or more years as US Best Managed Companies. The award serves to commend the accomplishments of private companies in the US and their dedicated management teams. This marks Coke Florida's fourth consecutive year being honored as a US Best Managed Company, emphasizing their commitment to excellence.
news.headline: Coca-Cola Beverages Florida named 2025 US Best Managed Company Gold Standard Winner, honored for 4+ years. Deloitte & Wall Street Journal sponsor the award.

news.summary: Cyberlux Corporation, a prominent provider of cutting-edge defense technologies and unmanned systems, has recently appointed Brigadier General Steven M. Anderson (Ret.) to its Defense Board of Advisors. This strategic move is aimed at leveraging General Anderson's extensive experience to enhance the company's Global Integrated Services (GIS) strategy. With a career spanning over thirty years, General Anderson will be instrumental in shaping Cyberlux's global logistics, sustainment, and infrastructure support capacities. This step signifies Cyberlux's commitment to strengthening its defense capabilities and expanding its reach in the industry.
news.headline: Cyberlux Corp appoints Brig Gen Steven Anderson (Ret) to Defense Board of Advisors, guiding GIS strategy. #Cyberlux #OTC #CYBL

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global economy is forecasted to grow by 5.6% in 2022, a notable increase from the 4.9% growth seen in 2021. This growth is being driven primarily by advanced economies, which are expected to expand by 4.8%, while emerging markets and developing economies are projected to grow by 6.4%. However, these figures come with a warning of ongoing risks and uncertainties, including the impact of COVID-19 variants, supply chain disruptions, and inflationary pressures. It is crucial for policymakers to remain vigilant and implement measures to support robust and inclusive growth in the face of these challenges.
news.headline: JBS sees profit rise due to strong chicken sales amid beef decline.

news.summary: In Rye, NY on May 14, 2025, the Board of Trustees of GAMCO Natural Resources, Gold & Income Trust (NYSE:GNT) has decided to continue paying monthly cash distributions. In 2025, distributions to common shareholders are anticipated to be mainly classified as a return of capital for tax purposes.

news.summary: Montana recently joined the ranks of states supporting rare disease advocacy as the 32nd state to establish a dedicated council. This milestone was achieved with the help of the National Organization for Rare Disorders (NORD®). Governor Greg Gianforte signed House Bill 943 into law on May 14, 2025, marking a significant step forward in addressing rare diseases in the state. The council created by this bill will play a crucial role in advancing awareness and support for individuals impacted by rare diseases in Montana.
news.headline: Montana sets up Rare Disease Advisory Council with support from NORD, becoming 32nd state to do so. Governor signed Bill 943.

news.summary: Altius Minerals Corporation announced that all resolutions at its Annual General Meeting on May 14, 2025 were approved by shareholders. This includes the election of directors Nicole Adshead-Bell, Teresa Conway, John Baker, Brian Dalton, Anna El-Erian, André Gaumond, Roger Lace, Fred Mifflin, and Jamie Strauss, all receiving over 95% votes in favor. The voting participation rate was also high. Altius Minerals Corporation trades on the TSX under ALS and on the OTCQX under ATUSF.
news.headline: Altius reports all resolutions at annual meeting approved by shareholders, directors elected with votes over 95%.

news.summary: In Rye, New York, on May 14, 2025, the Board of Trustees of GAMCO Global Gold, Natural Resources & Income Trust (NYSE American: GGN) announced their decision to continue paying monthly cash distributions. The Board declared cash distributions of $0.03 per share for July, August, and September 2025. It was noted that the Fund may exceed its distributable earnings with these distributions. For the year 2025, it is anticipated that distributions to common shareholders will mainly be considered as a return of capital for tax purposes.
news.headline: GAMCO Global Gold, Natural Resources & Income Trust to pay $0.03/share monthly, totaling $0.09 for July, August, September 2025.

news.summary: In Stamford, Connecticut on May 14, 2025, Academic Year in America (AYA) announced the Edwards family from Trenton, Kentucky, as the Host Family of the Year for 2024-2025. news.summary: In the latest report, it has been revealed that 5,000 people lost their jobs due to the closure of several factories in the region. This decision was made by the company in an effort to cut costs amidst the economic downturn. Additionally, the company's profits saw a significant decrease of 30% compared to the previous year, amounting to a total of $10 million. The CEO stated that this was a necessary step to ensure the long-term sustainability of the business. The affected employees will receive severance packages and job placement assistance to help them transition to new employment opportunities. The closure of these factories has also raised concerns about the impact on the local community and economy.

Analysts suggest that these positive trends in the labor market are indicative of a strengthening economy.

news.summary: EMRELIS has been authorized as the pioneering therapy specifically for individuals with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who have undergone prior treatment and demonstrate notable high levels of c-Met protein. These patients typically encounter a grim prognosis and confront restricted treatment alternatives. It is noteworthy that lung cancer persists as the primary contributor to cancer-related fatalities globally.
news.headline: U.S. FDA approves EMRELIS for previously treated advanced NSCLC patients with high c-Met protein overexpression.

news.summary: UrVenue, a top booking technology provider for hospitality experiences, was recently recognized for revolutionizing how resorts handle and profit from bookable experiences. The company's innovative Property Experience Management System, known as PXMS, has received acclaim for its transformative impact on the industry. This honor was announced in Las Vegas on May 14, 2025. UrVenue's platform is changing the way resorts manage their offerings, leading to an increase in revenue and enhanced customer satisfaction.
news.headline: UrVenue wins "Overall Reservation Solution Provider of the Year" award at TravelTech Breakthrough Awards. LAS VEGAS, May 14, 2025.

news.summary: XRP has surged by 65% in a month, rising from a low of $1.61 to $2.65 by May 14, driven by whale accumulation and easing US-China trade tensions. Technical analysis suggests a potential 45% rally, with a target near $3.69 by June, supported by exponential moving averages. The liquidation heatmap indicates a key resistance level at $2.68, with potential liquidations of $17.33 million, and a breakout could drive the price towards $2.87. A symmetrical triangle pattern suggests long-term targets of $5.24 and even $17 based on Fibonacci projections. It is advised for readers to conduct their own research before making investment decisions.
news.headline: XRP surged 65% in a month to $2.65, eyeing $2.87 resistance. Technicals predict a potential rally to $3.69, reaching $5.24 and possibly $17 long-term.
